inclusions.description: Glands and other organs for organo-therapeutic uses, dried or not; extracts of glands or other organs or their secretions; heparin and its salts; other human or animal substances prepared for therapeutic or prophylactic uses

Human blood; animal blood for therapeutic/prophylactic/diagnostic uses; antisera; other blood fractions and immunological products (whether or not modified or obtained by biotechnological processes); vaccines; toxins; cultures of micro-organisms; similar products

Immunological products: monoclonal antibodies (MAB), antibody fragments, antibody conjugates, interleukins, interferons (IFN), chemokines, tumor necrosis factors (TNF), growth factors (GF), hematopoietins, colony stimulating factors (CSF)
Medicaments (not in measured doses, not for retail sale): consisting of two or more constituents mixed together for therapeutic or prophylactic uses
Medicaments (in measured doses or packaged for retail sale): for therapeutic or prophylactic uses, for human or veterinary use

Wadding, gauze, bandages and similar articles (e.g. dressings, adhesive plasters, poultices) impregnated or coated with pharmaceutical substances or put up for retail sale for medical, surgical, dental or veterinary purposes

Pharmaceutical goods specified in Note 4 to Chapter 30 ONLY: surgical catgut; sterile suture materials; sterile tissue adhesives; sterile laminaria; haemostatics; X-ray opacifying preparations; diagnostic reagents for patient administration; placebos; clinical trial kits; dental cements; bone cements; first-aid boxes; chemical contraceptives (hormones/spermicides); lubricant gels for medical use; waste pharmaceuticals; ostomy appliances

inclusions: Subheadings 3003.60 / 3004.60 — Antimalarial medicaments containing artemisinin or specific antimalarial active principles (amodiaquine, artemether, artesunate, chloroquine, dihydroartemisinin, lumefantrine, mefloquine, piperaquine, pyrimethamine, sulfadoxine)

exclusions.condition: Foods or beverages including dietetic/diabetic/fortified foods, food supplements, tonic beverages, mineral waters — EXCEPT nutritional preparations for intravenous administration
exclusions.action: Redirect to Section IV (Chapters 16–24) — e.g., food supplements → 21.06; mineral waters → 22.01/22.02

exclusions.condition: Products containing nicotine intended to assist tobacco use cessation (tablets, chewing gum, transdermal patches with nicotine)
exclusions.action: Redirect to heading 24.04 — nicotine replacement therapy products

exclusions.condition: Plasters specially calcined or finely ground for use in dentistry (dental plaster)
Aqueous distillates or aqueous solutions of essential oils, suitable for medicinal uses
exclusions.action: Redirect to heading 33.01 — essential oil distillates/solutions

exclusions.condition: Preparations of headings 33.03 to 33.07 (perfumery, cosmetics, toiletry preparations) — even if they have therapeutic or prophylactic properties
exclusions.action: Redirect to headings 33.03–33.07 — cosmetics/toiletries always stay in Ch.33 regardless of medicinal claims

exclusions.condition: Soap or other products of heading 34.01 containing added medicaments
exclusions.action: Redirect to heading 34.01 — medicated soap stays in Ch.34

exclusions.condition: Preparations with a basis of plaster for use in dentistry
Blood albumin NOT prepared for therapeutic or prophylactic uses (i.e., industrial-grade blood albumin)
exclusions.action: Redirect to heading 35.02 — albumin for non-pharmaceutical uses (e.g., food processing, laboratory reagents)

exclusions.condition: Diagnostic reagents of heading 38.22 (in vitro diagnostic reagents, laboratory test kits — NOT administered to patient)
exclusions.action: Redirect to heading 38.22 — in-vitro diagnostics not administered to patient go to Ch.38, NOT 30.06(d)

classification_rules.description: Chapter 30 applies to pharmaceutical products for therapeutic, prophylactic or diagnostic use. However, many products with health claims are EXCLUDED: food supplements/health foods → Ch.21; cosmetics/toiletries with medicinal properties → Ch.33; medicated soap → Ch.34; nicotine replacement products → Ch.24.04; in-vitro diagnostic reagents not administered to patient → Ch.38.22. The determining factor is: Is the product primarily a pharmaceutical? Or primarily a food/cosmetic/soap?

classification_rules.rule: Note 2 — Definition of 'immunological products' for heading 30.02
classification_rules.description: For heading 30.02, 'immunological products' means peptides and proteins (OTHER THAN goods of heading 29.37) directly involved in regulating immunological processes. Specifically includes: Monoclonal antibodies (MAB), antibody fragments, antibody conjugates and antibody fragment conjugates; Interleukins; Interferons (IFN); Chemokines; Certain tumor necrosis factors (TNF); Growth factors (GF); Hematopoietins; Colony stimulating factors (CSF). Note: hormones of heading 29.37 are excluded from 30.02 immunologicals.

Critical distinction between 30.03 (not retail, not measured doses) and 30.04 (retail/measured doses): (a) Treated as UNMIXED: (1) unmixed products dissolved in water; (2) ALL goods of Chapter 28 or 29; (3) simple vegetable extracts of heading 13.02, merely standardised or dissolved in a solvent. (b) Treated as MIXED: (1) colloidal solutions and suspensions (except colloidal sulphur); (2) vegetable extracts from mixtures of vegetable materials; (3) salts and concentrates from evaporating natural mineral waters. This matters because 30.03 requires 'mixed' medicaments, while 30.04 covers any medicament (mixed or not) put up for retail in measured doses.

classification_rules.rule: Note 4 — Heading 30.06 is a mandatory capture list (eo nomine provision)
classification_rules.description: Heading 30.06 applies ONLY to its specific listed items and CANNOT be assigned to any other heading. The list is exhaustive: (a) Sterile surgical catgut, similar sterile suture materials (including sterile absorbable surgical/dental yarns) and sterile tissue adhesives for surgical wound closure; (b) Sterile laminaria and sterile laminaria tents; (c) Sterile absorbable haemostatics; sterile adhesion barriers (absorbable or not); (d) X-ray opacifying preparations AND diagnostic reagents to be administered to the patient (unmixed in measured doses OR mixed); (e) Placebos and blinded clinical trial kits in measured doses; (f) Dental cements and fillings; bone reconstruction cements; (g) First-aid boxes and kits; (h) Chemical contraceptives based on hormones (29.37) or spermicides; (ij) Lubricant gels for surgical operations or physical examinations or as coupling agent for medical instruments; (k) Waste/expired pharmaceuticals; (l) Ostomy appliances (colostomy, ileostomy, urostomy pouches) cut to shape with adhesive wafers.

classification_rules.rule: Key boundary: 30.02 vs 38.22 — administered to patient vs in-vitro
classification_rules.description: CRITICAL DISTINCTION: (a) Diagnostic reagents ADMINISTERED TO THE PATIENT (e.g., contrast media for X-ray, MRI contrast agents) → heading 30.06(d); (b) Diagnostic reagents used IN VITRO (laboratory test kits, test strips, PCR kits, blood glucose strips, pregnancy tests) → heading 38.22. The single test: is the reagent/substance given to the patient or used in a lab on a sample?

classification_rules.rule: Key boundary: 30.03 vs 30.04 — retail form determines classification
classification_rules.description: Both 30.03 and 30.04 cover medicaments for therapeutic/prophylactic use. The critical difference: 30.04 = put up in MEASURED DOSES or in forms/packings for RETAIL SALE (tablets, capsules, ampoules, sachets for individual use). 30.03 = bulk medicaments NOT in measured doses and NOT for retail sale (bulk APIs, hospital bulk preparations). A product shifts from 30.03 to 30.04 purely by its presentation/packaging.

classification_rules.rule: Key boundary: cosmetics with therapeutic claims stay in Ch.33
classification_rules.description: Note 1(e) is categorical: preparations of headings 33.03–33.07 go to Ch.33 EVEN IF they have therapeutic or prophylactic properties. This means: medicated shampoo (anti-dandruff) → 33.05; anti-acne face wash → 33.04; fluoride toothpaste → 33.06; sunscreen → 33.04. The product's PRIMARY classification as a cosmetic/toiletry overrides any medicinal claim.

classification_rules.rule: Subheading Note 1 — Provisions for 3002.13 and 3002.14 (monoclonal antibodies)
classification_rules.description: For subheadings 3002.13 and 3002.14 (which cover specific monoclonal antibody products under heading 30.02), the following are treated as UNMIXED: (a) pure products whether or not with impurities; (b1) dissolved in water/solvents; (b2) with added stabiliser; (b3) with any other additive. This means MAB products with additives (stabilisers, carriers, etc.) are still classifiable here.

Subheadings 3003.60 and 3004.60 cover medicaments containing artemisinin (INN) for oral ingestion combined with other active ingredients, OR containing any of these antimalarial active principles (alone or combined): amodiaquine, artelinic acid/salts, artenimol, artemotil, artemether, artesunate, chloroquine, dihydroartemisinin, lumefantrine, mefloquine, piperaquine, pyrimethamine, or sulfadoxine.

classification_rules.rule: Nutritional preparations for IV administration → Chapter 30, NOT Section IV
classification_rules.description: Note 1(a) creates an important exception: nutritional preparations for INTRAVENOUS administration (e.g., parenteral nutrition solutions, IV glucose, IV amino acid solutions, total parenteral nutrition/TPN) belong to Chapter 30, NOT to Section IV (food chapters). The IV route of administration elevates them to pharmaceutical status.
